metadata.name: lcwa
metadata.summary: LCWA (Loss Curvature Weighted Averaging) implementation for PyTorch

classes.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.hessian.hessian

methods.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.hessian.hessian.__init__
documentation:
model: the model that needs Hessain information
criterion: the loss function
data: a single batch of data, including inputs and its corresponding labels
dataloader: the data loader including bunch of batches of data

methods.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.hessian.hessian.eigenvalues
documentation:
compute the top_n eigenvalues using power iteration method
maxIter: maximum iterations used to compute each single eigenvalue
tol: the relative tolerance between two consecutive eigenvalue computations from power iteration
top_n: top top_n eigenvalues will be computed

methods.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.hessian.hessian.trace
documentation:
compute the trace of hessian using Hutchinson's method
maxIter: maximum iterations used to compute trace
tol: the relative tolerance

methods.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.hessian.hessian.density
documentation:
compute estimated eigenvalue density using stochastic lanczos algorithm (SLQ)
iter: number of iterations used to compute trace
n_v: number of SLQ runs

The class used to compute :
    i) the top 1 (n) eigenvalue(s) of the neural network
    ii) the trace of the entire neural network
    iii) the estimated eigenvalue density

functions.name: lcwa.lcwa.bn_update
documentation: Update BatchNorm statistics.

documentation: Forward pass of the model.

documentation: Convert model parameters to a single vector.

documentation: Update model parameters from vector.

methods.name: lcwa.lcwa.LCWA.update_parameters
documentation: Update LCWA parameters.

documentation: LCWA update procedure.

methods.name: lcwa.lcwa.LCWA.add_two_models
documentation: Add parameters of two models with weights.

methods.name: lcwa.lcwa.LCWA.set_requires_grad_for_specific_layers
documentation: Set requires_grad for specific layers.

documentation: Compute decay factor.

methods.name: lcwa.lcwa.LCWA.getA_Hessian_theory
documentation: Calculate Hessian-based weight for LCWA.

Loss Curvature Weighted Averaging (LCWA) implementation.

Args:
    model (nn.Module): The neural network model to be trained
    device (torch.device): Device to run the model on (CPU or GPU)
    train_loader (DataLoader): DataLoader for training data
    criterion (nn.Module): Loss function
    layer_name (str): Name of the layer to apply LCWA (e.g., 'classifier', 'linear', 'mlp_head')

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.group_product
documentation:
the inner product of two lists of variables xs,ys
\:param xs:
\:param ys:
\:return:

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.group_add
documentation:
params = params + update\*alpha
\:param params: list of variable
\:param update: list of data
\:return:

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.normalization
documentation:
normalization of a list of vectors
return: normalized vectors v

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.get_params_grad
documentation: get model parameters and corresponding gradients

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.hessian_vector_product
documentation:
compute the hessian vector product of Hv, where
gradsH is the gradient at the current point,
params is the corresponding variables,
v is the vector.

functions.name: lcwa.pyhessianv2.utils.orthnormal
documentation:
make vector w orthogonal to each vector in v_list.
afterwards, normalize the output w
